partial_findings.content: The report will be structured into the following sections: Introduction, Methodology, Findings, Discussion, Conclusion, References, Executive Summary, and Presentation.
The report format will include sections for Introduction, Methodology, Findings, Discussion, Conclusion, References, Executive Summary, and Presentation.
The Introduction section will provide background information and state the research objectives clearly.
The Methodology section will detail the research design, data collection methods, and analysis techniques used in the study.
The Findings section will present the data collected, including visuals such as charts and graphs to illustrate key points.
The Discussion section will interpret the findings, linking them back to the research objectives and existing literature.

phase_outputs.plan:
The to-do list for the reporting phase has been successfully created. Here’s a summary of the plan:

### Reporting Phase Summary
1. Define the report structure and format.
2. Gather all findings and organize them by themes.
3. Draft the Introduction section.
4. Draft the Methodology section.
5. Draft the Findings section, including visuals.
6. Draft the Discussion section.
7. Draft the Conclusion section.
8. Draft the References section.
9. Review the draft for clarity and coherence.
10. Revise sections based on the review.
11. Finalize the report formatting.
12. Create an Executive Summary.
13. Prepare a presentation based on the report.

This structured approach will ensure a comprehensive and well-documented report that effectively communicates the research findings.
